mainEntity.name: What documents are needed for overseas direct investment compliance ca?

acceptedAnswer.text: Key documents include: Certificate of Incorporation, PAN, share subscription/transfer agreement, valuation report, board resolution, FIRC from the bank, and details of the foreign investor. We provide a personalised checklist after the initial consultation.

mainEntity.name: How long does overseas direct investment compliance ca take?

acceptedAnswer.text: Standard filings: 7-10 working days. Compounding applications: 15-21 working days for preparation (RBI processing takes additional 60-90 days). Contact us at +91-99622 60333 for your specific timeline.

mainEntity.name: What happens if FEMA compliance is not done on time?

acceptedAnswer.text: FEMA contraventions attract penalty up to 3x the transaction amount under Section 13, plus Rs 5,000/day for continuing contravention. Compounding with RBI is possible but becomes more expensive with delay. Early compliance is always advisable.
